title: artifact-ui — How This Tool Works
description: artifact-ui is an MCP tool that turns a JSON 'system_spec' into an interactive, multi-view HTML diagram (Architecture, Layers, Sequences, Code Detail, Matrix, Components). The Architecture view's layout and SVG drawing run entirely client-side in arch_engine.js — Python only validates/resolves the spec and emits it as JSON. Groups are collapsible nodes: a group's 'members' ARE its drill-down content (real functions/files/etc), with no separate nested spec to keep in sync — this page is itself built that way, so every group below has its own Code Detail entry made of the real functions it groups. 'clone_of' lets a group's whole member subtree be reused under a new id-prefix. This page documents the tool's own current architecture, including its client-side layout engine and its two dev-only test suites.

nodes.label: AI Agent
nodes.description: Claude (or another MCP client) calling the artifact-ui tools to explain code, plans, or architecture.

nodes.label: render_artifact handler
nodes.description: The render_artifact branch of call_tool(). Renders via the chosen template, wraps the HTML with the Save-button/window.artifact runtime, writes both the ephemeral /tmp/{id}/{id}.html + .spec.json, and — if 'path' is given — also writes the persistent .html + sibling '<name>.spec.json' (no Save-button injected there).
nodes.signature: async def call_tool(name, arguments) -> list[TextContent]  # 'render_artifact' branch
nodes.code_snippet:
rendered_html = renderer(data)
html = inject_runtime(rendered_html, artifact_id)
(artifact_dir / f"{artifact_id}.html").write_text(html, encoding="utf-8")
(artifact_dir / f"{artifact_id}.spec.json").write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")

persist_path = arguments.get("path")
if persist_path:
    dest = Path(persist_path)
    dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    dest.write_text(rendered_html, encoding="utf-8")
    spec_path_for(dest).write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")

nodes.label: inject_runtime()
nodes.description: Wraps rendered HTML with window.artifact.submit() (for interactive mode) and an injected 'Save' button that downloads the page as a standalone .html file. Only applied to the ephemeral /tmp copy, never to a persistent 'path' destination.
nodes.signature: def inject_runtime(html: str, artifact_id: str) -> str

nodes.label: spec_path_for()
nodes.description: Maps an artifact's .html path to its sibling spec path, e.g. index.html -> index.spec.json.

nodes.label: save_artifact()
nodes.description: MCP tool. Promotes an ephemeral /tmp artifact (html + spec.json, if present) to a persistent path, creating directories as needed.

nodes.label: get_artifact_spec()
nodes.description: MCP tool. Reads back the sibling '<name>.spec.json' for a persistent artifact's .html path, so an agent can edit and re-render it in place.

nodes.label: serve_artifact()
nodes.description: FastAPI route serving the /tmp preview, reading from the per-artifact directory layout.

nodes.label: _maybe_hot_reload()
nodes.description: Called at the top of list_tools() and call_tool(). If the watched-source mtime advanced, purges system_spec/template_loader/system_spec_examples/design_system from sys.modules, re-imports them fresh, and refreshes BUILTIN_RENDERERS['system_spec'] — so edits take effect on the next tool call without restarting the process.

nodes.label: _watched_source_mtime()
nodes.description: Walks _HOT_RELOAD_ROOTS (template_loader.py, system_spec_examples.py, design_system.py, system_spec/) and returns the newest mtime across *.py, *.js (arch_engine fragments), AND *.json (bundled examples) — so editing the client-side engine or an example fixture also triggers a hot reload, not just Python edits.

nodes.label: list_tools()
nodes.description: MCP protocol handler returning the 4 tool schemas (get_example, render_artifact, save_artifact, get_artifact_spec). Calls _maybe_hot_reload() first, then template_loader.build_tool_description() for render_artifact's description.

nodes.label: get_example()
nodes.description: MCP tool. The get_example branch of call_tool() — returns EXAMPLES[name] or an error listing available names.

nodes.label: _free_stale_port()
nodes.description: Called once at startup. Kills any leftover process still bound to port 8765 (e.g. an orphaned session from a crashed/replaced server), so a fresh process never needs a manual restart first.

nodes.label: parse_spec()
nodes.description: Resolves any clone_of groups (materializing their member subtrees under a new id-prefix) BEFORE validating. Then checks node/edge/group shape, that every group member is a known node-or-group id, and the single-parent invariant (a node/group may belong to at most one group) — required for collapse/expand to stay unambiguous. Reused for the top-level spec AND for each group's own subtree spec built by render_code_detail_html.

nodes.label: render_system_spec()
nodes.description: Top-level orchestrator. Calls render_diagram_block() once for the top-level Architecture tab, then renders every other tab and inserts Layers/Sequences/Code Detail/Changes tabs only when the spec has content for them. Layout and SVG drawing are NOT done here — this just emits the validated spec + style tables as JSON for arch_engine.js to consume client-side.

nodes.label: render_diagram_block()
nodes.description: Shared builder for one architecture-diagram unit, parameterized by id_prefix — used identically by the top-level Architecture tab AND by every Code Detail module panel. Builds the filter bar, the Animate dropdown, the Route (edge-style) dropdown, the detail/group/legend panels, and a JSON payload of {spec, styles} dropped into a <script type="application/json"> tag next to an empty 'sys-mount' div — arch_engine.js (bundled once per page, into _ARCH_ENGINE_JS) finds that div and does the actual layout + drawing.
nodes.signature: def render_diagram_block(spec: dict, id_prefix: str = "") -> str

nodes.label: build_node_sequences()
nodes.description: Maps each node id to the sequences that touch it (id, label, first_step), feeding render_detail_panels' 'Appears in Sequences' section.

nodes.label: build_animate_block()
nodes.description: Builds the 'Animate' dropdown (with a 'None' option first to hide the scrubber entirely) plus one hidden-by-default timeline block per sequence.

nodes.label: build_edge_style_block()
nodes.description: Builds the 'Route' dropdown (Curve / Grid) that lets the Architecture tab switch the client-side edge-rendering mode without touching node/group positions — wired to window.sysEdgeStyleChange in 40_bootstrap.js.

nodes.label: render_filter_bar()
nodes.description: Renders the Architecture tab's toggle buttons: node kind, change status, edge kind, and group. The client JS (assets_filters.py) treats 'all deselected' as 'fade everything of that type' rather than 'show everything'.

nodes.label: render_detail_panels()
nodes.description: Renders the per-node sidebar detail panel: description, signature, code snippet, clickable hyperlinks to peer nodes under Calls/Sends and Receives From, and (when node_sequences is passed) an 'Appears in Sequences' section.

nodes.label: render_group_panels()
nodes.description: Every group is a collapsible node, so its placeholder box needs a sidebar panel too — same mechanism as a real node's, just showing the group's kind and its own member list (e.g. this very panel, for the group you just clicked).

nodes.label: render_legend()
nodes.description: Renders the sidebar legend, scoped to only the node-kinds and edge-kinds actually used in this spec.

nodes.label: render_code_detail_html()
nodes.description: Gets an entry in the Code Detail tab's module dropdown for EVERY group in the spec (not just specially-authored ones) — there's no separate 'detail' concept anymore; a group's own members already ARE its drill-down content. For each group, walks its real subtree (_collect_subtree), validates it as its own sub-spec, and renders it with render_diagram_block() — the exact same diagram component as Architecture, just scoped. This Code Detail tab is itself rendered this way.

nodes.label: _collect_subtree()
nodes.description: BFS over one group's 'members', following nested group ids and collecting real leaf nodes, then keeps only the edges whose both endpoints fall inside that leaf set.

nodes.label: render_changes_html()
nodes.description: Renders the Changes tab from spec['nodes']. Since group members are now ordinary top-level nodes (no separate detail namespace to merge in), a status='modified' node nested inside a collapsed group is automatically included — no extra plumbing needed. Only invoked when at least one node has status added/modified/deleted.

nodes.label: render_sequence_html()
nodes.description: Renders the Sequences tab: a dropdown over each sequence's swimlane diagram plus a scrub/play timeline (render_timeline_widget) and a click-to-reveal panel per step for any example/example_before/example_after data.

nodes.label: render_timeline_widget()
nodes.description: Shared scrub/play timeline control parameterized by target_kind ('arch' or 'seq') so the same client JS drives both the Architecture tab's overlay and each Sequences-tab diagram.

nodes.label: render_matrix_html()
nodes.description: Renders the Matrix tab — a dependency matrix of every node against every other node.

nodes.label: render_component_list_html()
nodes.description: Renders the Components tab — a flat, searchable list of every node with its description and tech.

nodes.label: 00_constants_and_utils.js
nodes.description: Shared layout constants (PAD, NODE_H, V_GAP, H_GAP, GROUP_PAD_X/TOP/BOTTOM) and small utils (truncate, maxCharsFor, edgeGoesBackward). edgeGoesBackward(sp,dp) flags an edge with no usable inter-layer gap below its source (same-row or true feedback edge) so 30_draw.js can anchor it on the box's side instead of forcing it through top/bottom.

nodes.label: 10_layout.js
nodes.description: findBackEdgeSet (cycle breaking) + orderLayersByBarycenter (crossing reduction) + layoutFlat/layoutHierarchy: recursive bottom-up-size/top-down-translate layout so an expanded group's box is sized to fit its real members and the rest of the graph shifts minimally around it. No external layout library (e.g. dagre/ELK) — hand-built Sugiyama-style by design.

nodes.label: 15_label_layout.js
nodes.description: Two secondary geometry passes, both computed for ALL edges before anything is drawn: computeEdgeLabelBoxes resolves overlapping edge-label boxes (sorted top-to-bottom, only ever pushed down). computeEdgeAnchorOffsets spreads edges that share a collapsed group's box across that box's width when they came from/to genuinely different hidden members, AND nudges an aggregated edge off-center whenever it shares its exact (from,to) pair with another differently-kinded edge.

nodes.label: 20_visibility.js
nodes.description: buildParentMap/isVisible/drawnAncestorFor/getVisibleGraph: resolves which nodes/groups are currently visible given an expandedSet, and redirects every edge endpoint hidden inside a collapsed group to that group's placeholder. Preserves each edge's original (pre-redirect) endpoint as _origFrom/_origTo, so 15_label_layout.js's fan-out (and 25_edge_aggregation.js's click panel) can tell apart edges that came from different hidden members.

nodes.label: 25_edge_aggregation.js
nodes.description: aggregateEdges(): merges edges that resolve to the exact same (from, to, kind) into one unlabeled, clickable line, run before computeEdgeAnchorOffsets/computeEdgeLabelBoxes ever see the edge list. Returns a parallel viaIndexOf so via-lane lookups (keyed by the pre-aggregation index) still resolve correctly.

nodes.label: 27_orthogonal_routing.js
nodes.description: Grid (orthogonal) edge routing: every hop between two consecutive points is built as an escape -> lane -> escape elbow, resolved by routeHopsAvoidingOverlap, which checks each new hop's actual geometry against every previously placed hop and real obstacle (generate-and-verify, not a heuristic envelope), escalating from a same-height gap bend up through a grown side lane to an obstacle/placed-hop-aware detour (obstacleSafeDetour / escapeEverythingDetour, both deriving safe escape heights from the real obstacle list via freeYBands rather than assuming a fixed V_GAP). computeOrthogonalAnchorOffsets spreads every edge touching a node (from OR to, one shared pool) across its width so grid mode's straight vertical runs don't coincide at a shared anchor.

nodes.label: 30_draw.js
nodes.description: drawDiagram(): runs aggregateEdges() first, then draws group boxes, edges (curve() or, in grid mode, orthogonalPathD(routeEdgesOrthogonally(...)) — same anchor points, only the path geometry differs), and nodes/collapsed-group placeholders into the SVG. A back-edge (edgeGoesBackward) anchors on the box's right side instead of top/bottom, with curveSide() bulging outward for curve mode, so it never has to fight an ordinary forward edge for the same inter-layer territory. drawOverlay(): pre-renders the hidden traveling-pulse paths used by the Animate timeline, following the same routingMode and back-edge anchoring as the static edges.
nodes.signature: function drawDiagram(svg, visible, styles, layout, idPrefix, routingMode)

nodes.label: 40_bootstrap.js
nodes.description: Mounts every 'sys-mount' div on the page by reading its sibling JSON payload, then runs getVisibleGraph -> layoutHierarchy -> drawDiagram for the first paint. Exposes window.sysToggleGroup (expand/collapse), window.sysClick (opens a node/group's detail panel), and window.sysEdgeStyleChange (switches the Route dropdown between curve/grid and re-renders).

nodes.label: system_spec_examples
nodes.description: EXAMPLES dict returned by get_example, built by loading every JSON file under system_spec/examples/*.json — the one source of truth shared with the arch_engine test suite's regression fixtures, so there's no hand-duplicated example data anywhere.

nodes.label: template_loader
nodes.description: Builds the render_artifact tool description (use cases, schema, vocab, sequence example/animation notes) shown to the agent.

nodes.label: design_system
nodes.description: Shared HTML page shell, CSS tokens, and the page_wrapper() used by every rendered artifact.

nodes.label: Node
nodes.description: A box in the Architecture diagram. 'kind' picks its icon/color (service, db, queue, external, module, function, class, file, package). Code-level fields (signature, code_snippet, previous_code_snippet, file_path, line_range, status) add the detail-panel snippet, location metadata, and Changes-tab entries.
nodes.code_snippet:
{
  "id": "orders",              // unique, referenced by edges/groups/sequences
  "label": "Order Service",
  "kind": "service",           // service|db|queue|external|module|function|class|file|package
  "description": "...",        // shown in the detail panel
  "tech": "Go",                // sub-label
  "owner": "team-name",
  "status": "stable",          // stable|experimental|deprecated|planned
                               // or added|modified|deleted -> Changes tab + colored border
  "tags": ["critical"],
  "signature": "func ...",      // code-level: shown above the snippet
  "code_snippet": "...",        // code-level: syntax-highlighted
  "previous_code_snippet": "...", // code-level: enables before/after diff
  "file_path": "src/...",
  "line_range": [10, 42]
}

nodes.label: Edge
nodes.description: An arrow from one node to another. 'kind' sets its color/style (calls, imports, depends, emits, subscribes, reads, writes, deploys, owns, returns, throws, overrides, implements, instantiates). 'async: true' or certain kinds render as dashed. If an endpoint is hidden inside a collapsed group, the edge is redirected to that group's box; if multiple redirected edges share that box but have different (from,to) pairs, computeEdgeAnchorOffsets spreads their anchor points; if two or more resolve to the exact identical (from,to,kind), aggregateEdges merges them into one unlabeled, clickable line instead — click it to see the real underlying connections.
nodes.code_snippet:
{
  "from": "ui",                // node id
  "to": "api",                  // node id
  "kind": "calls",
  "label": "REST",              // optional, shown on the arrow
  "async": false,               // optional -> dashed line
  "protocol": "HTTP"            // optional, informational
}

nodes.label: Group
nodes.description: A labeled, collapsible bounding box around a set of node and/or group ids. There is no separate 'detail' block — a group's own 'members' ARE what it expands to reveal. Every id may have at most one parent group, which is what makes collapse/expand unambiguous. A group must have either 0 or 2+ members. kind='layer' groups also become swim-lanes in the Layers tab.

nodes.label: Group.clone_of
nodes.description: Reuses an entire existing group's member subtree (nested groups, nodes, and the edges among/touching them) under a new id-prefix, instead of hand-duplicating it — useful for cells/shards/replicas/regions. The source group can't itself be a clone (no chaining), and every id nested under it must already be prefixed with the source group's own id, so the new ids can be derived mechanically.

nodes.label: Sequence
nodes.description: An ordered list of steps (from -> to, with a label) between node ids, rendered as a swimlane diagram in the Sequences tab and overlaid on the Architecture tab via its Animate control. Both support a scrub/play timeline that walks the steps as a traveling pulse, with a click-to-reveal panel for any step's example data.

nodes.label: tests/arch_engine
nodes.description: Property + regression suite (fast-check + Node's built-in test runner). harness.js concatenates the real arch_engine/*.js fragments and exposes their internal functions for direct testing, no DOM needed. Covers cycle-breaking, hierarchical group layout, edge-label overlap, edge aggregation, and grid-mode orthogonal routing — axis-alignment, obstacle avoidance, anchor preservation, and (against a generator scoped to realistic diagram density/forward-bias, since the router is a greedy per-edge search rather than a true global track allocator) that distinct edges' routed segments never collinearly overlap.

nodes.label: tests/interactions
nodes.description: DOM-level property + regression suite for the Architecture filter bar's toggle behavior, expand/collapse re-renders, and same-kind duplicate-edge aggregation. render_helper.js shells out to python3 to render real specs through the actual pipeline, so it always exercises current filter/draw logic, not a mock.

nodes.label: Artifact Storage
nodes.description: Ephemeral previews under /tmp/artifact_ui/{id}/{id}.html + {id}.spec.json, plus optional persistent paths (e.g. this index.html + index.spec.json) written when 'path' is given to render_artifact.

nodes.label: Browser Tab
nodes.description: Opened via webbrowser.open() to preview the rendered artifact. arch_engine.js executes here — layout and drawing happen client-side, on first paint and again on every expand/collapse toggle or Route/Animate control change.

edges.label: property + regression tests for grid routing
_origFrom/_origTo feed the click panel's member list

steps.label: wrap html with Save button + window.artifact (ephemeral copy only)

sequences.label: Iterate on a persistent artifact later
Edit code without restarting the server

steps.label: edit a file under system_spec/ (incl. arch_engine/*.js) or server.py
_watched_source_mtime(): max mtime across *.py, *.js, *.json in watched roots
build filter bar + Route/Animate dropdowns + JSON {spec, styles} payload + sys-mount div
browser mounts: getVisibleGraph -> layoutHierarchy -> drawDiagram (and again on every expand/collapse or Route change)

sequences.label: Render the Code Detail tab (this sequence)

steps.label: walk this group's own members (nested groups + leaf nodes) and edges among them
validate the resulting sub-spec — own id namespace, no separate 'detail' concept
render the SAME diagram type as Architecture, scoped to this module
steps.example_after: groups[].members = ["realFn1", "realFn2"]  // new: the group's members ARE the code

sequences.label: Author a group that drills into real code

steps.label: from/to must be a known node id
a group's members are real node/group ids directly — no nested 'detail' block; each id has at most one parent
or reuse another group's whole subtree under a new id-prefix instead of hand-authoring members
steps.example: {"id": "cell_b", "clone_of": "cell_a"}  // every nested id must be prefixed cell_a_...
